titleStmt.title: Letter from Terrence Duffin to his mother, Maria Duffin, 31 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Terrence Duffin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Terrence Duffin to his mother Maria Duffin, 31 December 1915. Terrence is writing to his mother to inform her that he is going on leave shortly and coming to see her in Ireland.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
D2109/9/4/C Dearest Mamma This is just a line to give
you the good news which I
have just got tonight that I
am to go on leave next week. I leave here on Friday
or Saturday - If Friday
I should be home on Sunday
the 9thbut if I leave on Satur-
day I probably won't be able
to cross till Monday 10th to 39
D2109/9/4/C Ireland. Anyhow
I will find out in London
when I arrive and let
you know. If stranded
in London I will get a
chance of seeing Celia— I will be glad of the change
as you can imagine for I
have been thronged lately! Tomorrow we move our
entire Bde. Hd. Qrs - Today I have had 40
D2109/9/4/C to find complete new billets
and could find no place
for the Staff but an empty
house without a stick of
furniture or means of cooking
tomorrows meals. I have
been on my knees to the R.E.
and have persuaded them
to stick up a stove of
sorts and have also tasked
the Divl. Interpreter to
try and find some 41
D2109/9/4/C chairs & tables. In a
day or two we may settle
down again! - Tonight at
11.20pm I am still wading
through paper in the office! Had a line and some
cigarettes from Emma and
have written back to let
her know I am alive &
well. Nothing else new to
relate meanwhile - Thanks 42
D2109/9/4/C for letters from yourself
Edmond, Ruth & Dorothy Best love and hope
to see you all soon. Ever Your own Terry 31/12/15
